pr_title: refactor(api): add tsconfig.spec.json plumbing for jest
title: apps/api/tsconfig.json is NOT updated to reference the new spec config.
body:
apps/api/tsconfig.json is NOT updated to reference the new spec config.

apps/mobile/tsconfig.json includes the spec path in its references array; apps/api/tsconfig.json does not. Without this, tsc --build (composite project graph) never visits the spec config. Only ts-jest picks it up via jest.config.cjs. The NX typecheck target and IDE incremental builds remain unaware, so the plumbing is half-wired.

Fix in apps/api/tsconfig.json — add ./tsconfig.spec.json alongside ./tsconfig.app.json in the references array, matching the mobile pattern exactly.

pr_title: refactor(api): add tsconfig.spec.json plumbing for jest
title: Every other package/app in this monorepo that ships a `tsconfig.spec.json` lists it in its root `tsconfig.json` references array:
body:
Every other package/app in this monorepo that ships a `tsconfig.spec.json` lists it in its root `tsconfig.json` references array:

| File | References spec? |
|---|---|
| `packages/database/tsconfig.json` | YES `./tsconfig.spec.json` |
| `packages/schemas/tsconfig.json` | YES `./tsconfig.spec.json` |
| `apps/mobile/tsconfig.json` | YES `./tsconfig.spec.json` |
| `apps/api/tsconfig.json` | **NO — missing** |

Without the reference, `tsc --build` (the `api:typecheck` NX target) never discovers `tsconfig.spec.json`. Test files continue to compile only via ts-jest's transform — the whole point of this PR was to make `tsc --build` type-check test files using the spec config, and that goal is not achieved until this reference is added.

The Verification table shows "TypeCheck | PASS | 5 from cache, 1 fresh" — the cache hit strongly suggests the `api` project was not invalidated because `apps/api/tsconfig.json` was untouched. The spec tsconfig can exist and jest passes, yet CI type-checking of test files remains silently absent.

**Required fix:** add `{ "path": "./tsconfig.spec.json" }` to the `references` array in `apps/api/tsconfig.json`:

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "files": [],
  "include": [],
  "references": [
    { "path": "./tsconfig.app.json" },
    { "path": "./tsconfig.spec.json" }
  ]
}
```

After adding it, run `pnpm exec tsc --build apps/api/tsconfig.json --force` to confirm the ~279 deferred errors surface as expected (not silently swallowed), then verify the NX typecheck target still reports PASS (it should, because `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` and `noUnusedLocals` are disabled in the spec).

---

title: apps/api/tsconfig.json is not updated
body:
_apps/api/tsconfig.json is not updated_

The new tsconfig.spec.json is never added to the root apps/api/tsconfig.json references array. In TypeScript composite/project-references mode, tsc --build (and nx run api:typecheck) walks the graph from the root tsconfig -- currently only tsconfig.app.json is reachable. Test files are therefore excluded from the nx typecheck build, meaning type errors in .test.ts files are invisible during CI typecheck.

The PR description itself reports Verdict: BLOCK / 1C from the Archon review, and the PR title says "3 files" while the diff shows only 2 -- the missing tsconfig.json change is almost certainly the Critical finding.

Fix: add `{ "path": "./tsconfig.spec.json" }` to the references array in apps/api/tsconfig.json alongside tsconfig.app.json.

pr_title: notion-bugfixing: Practice Hub UI polish & progress reports feature
title: Silent parse failure in `mapMonthlyReportRow` / `mapWeeklyReportRow` (both services)
body:
**1. Silent parse failure in `mapMonthlyReportRow` / `mapWeeklyReportRow` (both services)**

Switching from `.parse()` (throws) to `safeParse()` (returns `null`) on the detail mappers creates a silent data-corruption hole with no logging. The inconsistency is significant: a record that fails schema validation is a 500 in the list view (still uses `.parse()`) and an invisible 404 in the detail view. CLAUDE.md: "silent recovery without escalation is banned." At minimum, emit a `logger.warn` with the report ID and error before returning `null` — otherwise there is zero signal to detect that a DB record is corrupt.

See inline comments on `monthly-report.ts:235` and `weekly-report.ts:138`.

pr_title: notion-bugfixing: Practice Hub UI polish & progress reports feature
title: Fragile dark-mode detection in `practice/index.tsx
body:
**2. Fragile dark-mode detection in `practice/index.tsx`**

`const isDark = theme.background !== '#faf5ef'` compares against a magic hex value. If the light-mode background token changes, dark-mode detection silently inverts across the entire practice hub with no type or test warning. Use the theme system's own dark/light boolean. Beyond that, lines 38-57 define ~15 hardcoded hex values for semantic UI states (review, quiz, dictation, recite, history) that should live in the theme token map per CLAUDE.md's no-hardcoded-hex rule.

See inline comment on `practice/index.tsx:32`.

title: resolveTabShape` returns `'guardian'` during proxy sessions
body:
**3. `resolveTabShape` returns `'guardian'` during proxy sessions**

When `isParentProxy: true`, the layout renders the 5-tab guardian shape, which includes the **Own Learning** tab. That tab shows the parent's own learning content — surfacing it while the proxy banner says "you're viewing [child]" is a leaky context boundary. CLAUDE.md's tab-shape table puts "child on parent's account" in the learner shape (no own-learning); a proxy session is semantically that same context. The test at `_layout.test.tsx:722` validates the current behaviour as intentional, but it conflicts with the spec. Please either change to `'learner'` for proxy sessions or add a CLAUDE.md footnote explaining the deliberate choice.

See inline comment on `_layout.tsx:94`.

pr_title: notion-bugfixing: Practice Hub UI polish & progress reports feature
title: Issue 1: mapMonthlyReportRow and mapWeeklyReportRow: schema-validation failure silently returns 404.
body:
Issue 1: mapMonthlyReportRow and mapWeeklyReportRow: schema-validation failure silently returns 404.

Both functions now use safeParse and return null on failure. Their callers (getMonthlyReportForProfile, getMonthlyReportForParentChild, getWeeklyReportForProfile, getWeeklyReportForParentChild) pass that null straight through. The routes then call `if (!report) return notFound(c, 'Report not found')` which is indistinguishable from a genuine missing row.

Result: a DB row that fails schema validation (data corruption) returns HTTP 404 to the client, which treats it as report does not exist and shows a dead-end screen. The logger.warn fires but no alert is raised and the user has no path to recovery.

The list path (mapMonthlyReportSummary / mapWeeklyReportSummary) still uses .parse() which throws and gives a 500. So list and detail now have asymmetric failure semantics: a corrupted report appears normally in the list but 404s when tapped.

Fix: throw instead of returning null, or introduce a typed error the route catches and converts to 500. Inline comments left on both service files.

pr_title: notion-bugfixing: Practice Hub UI polish & progress reports feature
title: Issue 2: progress/weekly-report/_layout.tsx is missing unstable_settings.
body:
Issue 2: progress/weekly-report/_layout.tsx is missing unstable_settings.

The layout contains both index.tsx and [weeklyReportId].tsx. CLAUDE.md requires any new nested Expo Router layout that contains both an index screen and a deeper dynamic child to export unstable_settings = { initialRouteName: 'index' }. The sibling progress/reports/_layout.tsx (added in this same PR) correctly exports this. Without it, a cross-tab deep push to /progress/weekly-report/<id> synthesises a 1-deep stack and router.back() falls through to the tab root. Inline comment with a one-line fix suggestion on the file.

Issue 3: Hardcoded hex colours in practice/index.tsx usePracticeColors().

10+ hex literals (#2f9c6a, #12352a, #386dbe, #1a2e24, etc.) for activity-card backgrounds, borders, and accents are not in design-tokens.ts. The CLAUDE.md exception covers only Animation, Celebration, AnimatedSplash, and MentomateLogo components. These should be added to design-tokens.ts as semantic tokens (e.g. activityReview, activityQuiz, activityDictation, activityRecite) and consumed via useThemeColors(). Inline comment on lines 39-58.

WHAT LOOKS GOOD

- Auth scoping is correct: getMonthlyReportForProfile and getWeeklyReportForProfile filter on childProfileId = activeProfileId; parent-child paths double-guard with assertParentAccess() at both route and service layers.
- Integration tests are strong: progress-reports.integration.test.ts covers cross-account denial (profile A cannot read profile B gives 404), cross-family denial (unrelated parent gives 403), and happy paths with no internal mocks.
- Nudge consent-null fix is correct: treating null (no consent_states row, age >= 17) as allowed while blocking PENDING/WITHDRAWN/PARENTAL_CONSENT_REQUESTED is the right semantic. The break-test in nudge.test.ts documents the regression clearly.
- Drizzle result-shape hardening in book-suggestion-generation.ts is well-reasoned; throwing is better than the previous silent false default, and the new test covers the path.
- Timezone exception handling in isQuietHours is safe and the break-test verifies the nudge transaction commits before the bad-timezone path.
- unstable_settings is present on progress/reports/_layout.tsx; monthly report layout is correct.
- CLAUDE.md profile-shape documentation is a useful addition.

body:
CLAUDE.md Sweep when you fix rule requires that when a drift exists at 3+ locations, the fix must sweep all current sites in the same PR (or document a tracked deferred sweep). The bare db:generate reference appears in developer-facing docs that were not touched:

- README.md line 77: pnpm run db:generate

pr_title: refactor(schemas,mobile,api): expand AgeBracket to three-way and migrate personaFromBirthYear callers (PR-11)
title: File:** `apps/api/src/services/exchange-prompts.ts:27
body:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/exchange-prompts.ts:27`

```ts
export function resolveAgeBracket(birthYear?: number | null): AgeBracket {
  return birthYear == null ? 'adult' : computeAgeBracket(birthYear);  // still 'adult'
}
```

The PR sweeps every null-birthYear fallback to `'adolescent'` for defence-in-depth safety (`generate-round.ts:243`, `mentor-memory.tsx:100`, `relearn.tsx:138`, `consent.tsx`, `session-summary/[sessionId].tsx`). This function feeds both `getAgeVoice()` and `getSessionTypeGuidance()` for LLM exchanges — the most safety-critical callsite — and was not updated.

A learner with no `birthYear` on record will get `ADULT_VOICE` (adult communication style, no youth scaffolding) and adult brevity in homework mode, the opposite of the defence-in-depth intent. The original `personaFromBirthYear` also defaulted to `'learner'` (13–17 equivalent) for null, so this also breaks behavioural parity with the deleted code.

**Fix:**
```ts
return birthYear == null ? 'adolescent' : computeAgeBracket(birthYear);
```

Because this line was not modified in the diff, it cannot receive a direct inline comment — flagging here instead.

---

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-17 — Doc reconciliation bundle — Inngest observers, RLS plan refresh, CLAUDE.md persona rule + db:* commands, UX spec paths, SCHEMA-2 plan numbers, baseline-delta amendment, overlap-flags supersede. **Co-land with PR-27.**
title: The established observer pattern in `ask-classification-observe.ts` calls `captureException` (Sentry) in the schema drift path in addition to the logger call, so schema drift is traceable and alertabl
body:
The established observer pattern in `ask-classification-observe.ts` calls `captureException` (Sentry) in the schema drift path in addition to the logger call, so schema drift is traceable and alertable in Sentry, not just in log queries. The three new observers (`askGateDecisionObserve`, `askGateTimeoutObserve`, `emailBouncedObserve`) only call `logger.error`. If the Resend or sessions sender ever changes its payload shape, the drift will appear in structured logs but NOT in Sentry — meaning on-call won't get an alert unless actively watching the log aggregator.

Reference pattern from `ask-classification-observe.ts`:
```ts
captureException(
  new Error('[ask-gate-decision] invalid event payload — schema drift or bad event'),
  { extra: { issues: parseResult.error.issues, rawData: event.data } },
);
```

This should be applied to all three new schema_error paths for consistency with the established pattern.

title: The log message in `emailBouncedObserve` is always `'email.bounced.received'` even when `data.type` is `'email.complained'`. The event type is present in the `context.type` field, but anyone querying
body: The log message in `emailBouncedObserve` is always `'email.bounced.received'` even when `data.type` is `'email.complained'`. The event type is present in the `context.type` field, but anyone querying logs for `message = 'email.complained.received'` will get zero results. Makes complaint-specific log queries harder and creates potential confusion in dashboards/alerts built on the message name. Suggested fix: use the actual event type in the message name when available.

pr_title: Refresh learning flows and shared test utilities
title: Dead-end on final question while auto-save is in-flight (`play.tsx`)
body:
**3. Dead-end on final question while auto-save is in-flight (`play.tsx`)**
See inline comment on line 627-632. When the last question is answered, `submitRound({ navigateOnSuccess: false })` fires and sets `roundSubmittedRef.current = true`. If the user taps Continue before the save completes, `submitRound()` is called again, hits the ref guard, and returns silently -- no navigation, no error, no feedback. Add a `roundAutoSaveStarted` early-return so the `onSuccess` handler drives navigation instead.

title: play.tsx` — `handleSaveAndQuit` references `submitRound` before its `useCallback` declaration
body:
**`play.tsx` — `handleSaveAndQuit` references `submitRound` before its `useCallback` declaration**

`handleSaveAndQuit` is a plain `const` at ~line 239 but `submitRound` (a `useCallback`) is not declared until ~line 259. JavaScript `const` is not hoisted like `function`, so `no-use-before-define` will flag this. Safe at runtime (closure is only called on press, after full initialization) but is an ordering violation. Move `submitRound` above the event handlers that call it.

title: canEditChildPreferences` depends on `activeProfile` and `childProfile`, both from `useProfile()`. On first render, `profiles` may be empty and `activeProfile` may be null/undefined while the hook reso
body: `canEditChildPreferences` depends on `activeProfile` and `childProfile`, both from `useProfile()`. On first render, `profiles` may be empty and `activeProfile` may be null/undefined while the hook resolves. At that moment `canEditChildPreferences` evaluates to `false`, so the `useEffect` fires and redirects a legitimate owner away before their data loads. Fix: gate on `isLoading` from `useProfile()`.

title: play.tsx` — `handleSeeResults` / `handleOneMore` silent guard
body:
**`play.tsx` — `handleSeeResults` / `handleOneMore` silent guard**

Both handlers do `if (!roundAutoSaved) return;` and silently do nothing if called while the auto-save is still in flight. The JSX at line ~984 correctly gates the button section on `roundAutoSaved`, so the tap-window is narrow. But during the `roundAutoSaveStarted && !roundAutoSaved` window the guard produces a silent no-op with no feedback. Consider disabling the pressables or replacing with a spinner while `roundAutoSaveStarted && !roundAutoSaved`.

**`integration-mock-guard.test.ts` — filesystem walk instead of `git ls-files`**

The switch from `git ls-files` to a manual `readdirSync` walk means untracked and `.gitignore`d files can now be scanned. A local scratch `*.integration.test.ts` with an internal mock will fire the guard locally but never affect CI. `git ls-files` was more precise for the CI-enforcement intent. If the motivation was CI containers without git, document that constraint in the file.

**`use-sessions.ts` — `invalidateSessionDerivedQueries` broadens invalidation scope**

`useSetSessionInputMode` now invalidates `progress`, `dashboard`, `retention`, `language-progress`, and `resume-nudge` — queries that an input-mode toggle does not affect. Safe but causes unnecessary network chatter. If intentional ("always be fresh after any session mutation") add a short comment. If unintentional, scope `useSetSessionInputMode` back to just `['sessions']`.

title: Expo Router's tab press already pops to the stack root for the current tab and switches tabs for inactive ones. Calling `router.replace('/(app)/more')` inside the listener fires a second navigation ac
body: Expo Router's tab press already pops to the stack root for the current tab and switches tabs for inactive ones. Calling `router.replace('/(app)/more')` inside the listener fires a second navigation action on top of that default behaviour. The other four tabs only call `queryClient.invalidateQueries` in their listeners — the asymmetry is surprising. At best this is redundant; at worst it causes double-navigation on certain Expo Router versions. Remove the `router.replace`; default tab press + `refreshMoreTabQueries()` is sufficient.

pr_title: Refresh learning flows and shared test utilities
title: Toggling voice/text mode during an active session does not affect `progress`, `dashboard`, `retention`, `language-progress`, or `resume-nudge`. Adding `invalidateSessionDerivedQueries` here causes 5 u
body:
Toggling voice/text mode during an active session does not affect `progress`, `dashboard`, `retention`, `language-progress`, or `resume-nudge`. Adding `invalidateSessionDerivedQueries` here causes 5 unnecessary background refetches on every mode toggle. The original single `invalidateQueries` for `sessions` was correct for this mutation.

---

### Architectural note

**Cross-stack deep push without ancestor chain (CLAUDE.md rule)** — `LearnerScreen.tsx` now pushes directly to `/(app)/progress/[subjectId]` without first pushing `/(app)/progress`. CLAUDE.md requires pushing the full ancestor chain for cross-tab deep pushes so that `router.back()` does not fall through to the Tabs root. The `returnTo` param makes the explicit back-button press correct, but Android hardware back and iOS swipe-back use the native stack and will fall through to the Home tab rather than the Progress list. This is acceptable if the intent is "came from Home, back goes to Home" but should be documented explicitly.

---

### Test gap

**`useFocusEffect` mock calls once; refetch-on-focus path is untested** — The `progress.test.tsx` mock fires the callback once at mount, hitting the `hasFocusedOnceRef.current = true` early-return branch. The actual refetch path (second+ focus) is never exercised. A test simulating a second focus call would close this gap.

---

pr_title: Refresh learning flows and shared test utilities
title: [MEDIUM] Hard-timeout timer does not reset when the user retries while mutation is still pending — launch.tsx**
body:
**[MEDIUM] Hard-timeout timer does not reset when the user retries while mutation is still pending — launch.tsx**

Both useEffect timeout hooks depend only on [generateRound.isPending]. When the 30 s hard-timeout fires and the user presses Retry, the code calls setHardTimedOut(false) and startRound(). TanStack Query re-fires the mutation but does NOT change isPending (it stays true), so neither effect re-runs and no new timer is armed.

Consequence: a user who hits Retry on a slow network will never see the hard-timeout panel again; the only escape is the Cancel button. The same applies to the 20 s soft-nudge timer.

Fix: introduce a retryCount state variable and add it to both timeout-effect dep arrays, incrementing it inside the Retry press handler so the effects re-arm on each deliberate retry.

---

pr_title: Refresh learning flows and shared test utilities
title: [MEDIUM] Hardcoded English accessibility labels on final-question action buttons — play.tsx**
body:
**[MEDIUM] Hardcoded English accessibility labels on final-question action buttons — play.tsx**

Inline comment posted at lines 995-1002. Five accessibilityLabel props added in this PR use literal English strings instead of t():

- Line 1001: "See quiz results"
- Line 1015: "Start one more quiz"
- Line 1036: "Next question"
- Line 1052: "Dispute this answer"
- Line 1170: "Save progress and finish round"

The project ships 7 locale files. Screen-reader users on non-English locales always hear these labels in English. These should route through t() with new keys added to all locale files.

---

**[LOW] quiz.play.oneMore reused as the "keep playing" label in the quit modal — play.tsx**

Inline comment posted at lines 1148-1163. Post-round, quiz.play.oneMore means "start another quiz round." Inside the quit-confirm modal it means "stay in the current round." A user who has answered 3 of 5 questions, taps X, and sees One More as the primary CTA may read it as "finish then start one more" rather than "continue this round." Consider a dedicated key (quiz.play.continueRound). The zero-answers branch already uses quiz.play.keepPlaying consistently — the hasAnsweredQuestions branch should match that approach.

---

**[LOW] inngest.send failure after a successful DB write risks a double-complete on client retry — quiz.ts**

In POST /quiz/rounds/:id/complete, completeQuizRound commits the round to the DB first, then inngest.send fires the streak event. If inngest.send throws (network blip, Inngest outage), the client gets a 500 even though the round is already marked complete. On retry, completeQuizRound executes again on the same round and the streak event may double-fire.

Two paths forward: (1) confirm both completeQuizRound and streak-record are idempotent on (profileId, date) — then retry is harmless and just needs a comment; or (2) wrap inngest.send in a try/catch that emits a structured metric and returns 200 anyway (degraded: streak reconciled on next session).

---

**[LOW] registerLlmProviderFixture has no deregister path — llm-provider-fixtures.ts**

Inline comment posted at lines 201-207. The fixture calls registerProvider (mutating a module-level map) but there is no corresponding cleanup. If Jest runs multiple suites in the same worker, stale fixtures can leak across test files. Worth documenting whether the registry is reset by Jest module isolation, or returning a dispose() from registerLlmProviderFixture.

---

### Positives

- [BUG-929] Same-batch state reset in handleContinue is the right fix. Resetting answerState, freeTextAnswer, guessWhoCluesUsed, and the timer ref in the same React 18 batch closes the disabled-Pressable one-frame window without an extra render.
- shuffledOptions via useMemo (BUG-STALE-OPTIONS) is correct. A useEffect-based shuffle would leave a one-frame gap between question change and options update where a tap could record a stale answerGiven.
- Integration-mock guard (BUG-743) with an empty KNOWN_OFFENDERS is the right end state — signals full migration rather than a permanent carve-out list. The shrinking-allowlist assertion correctly future-proofs this.
- createInngestStepRunner threading through real callbacks for unconfigured steps is a useful pattern. Tests get real behavior for steps they do not care about and can selectively override only the steps under test.
- toClientSafeQuestions constructs a fresh object with only ClientQuizQuestion fields per branch, so no accidental field leakage even if QuizQuestion gains new sensitive fields later.
- useFocusEffect-based cache refresh in Progress screens follows the correct Expo Router / TanStack Query integration pattern, and the hasFocusedOnceRef guard prevents a redundant refresh on initial mount.

---

### Summary

Two medium findings (timeout reset, i18n accessibility labels) should be addressed before merge. The three low findings are safe to defer with tracking tickets. The core quiz-play logic, test infrastructure, and navigation improvements are well-engineered.

pr_title: fix(archon): expand brace shorthand in cleanup-extract.sh + PR-18 plan closure
title: Unquoted `$options` in `for` loop is subject to pathname expansion** (`.archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh`): With `IFS=','`, bash word-splits on commas correctly — but it also performs glob expansion
body:
**Unquoted `$options` in `for` loop is subject to pathname expansion** (`.archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh`): With `IFS=','`, bash word-splits on commas correctly — but it also performs glob expansion on each split token before the loop body runs. A path segment containing `*` or `[` would expand against the working directory. Safer alternative:

```bash
# Instead of:
local IFS=','
for opt in $options; do

# Use read -ra (prevents globbing, same IFS-split behavior):
local IFS=','
read -ra _opts <<< "$options"
for opt in "${_opts[@]}"; do
```

pr_title: fix(tests): remove internal mock cleanup debt
title: createSubject(profileId, name) always builds headers from SUBJECT_AUTH_USER_ID / SUBJECT_AUTH_EMAIL, even when passed otherProfileId. The new cross-profile test only ever calls it with the first user'
body: createSubject(profileId, name) always builds headers from SUBJECT_AUTH_USER_ID / SUBJECT_AUTH_EMAIL, even when passed otherProfileId. The new cross-profile test only ever calls it with the first user's ownerProfileId so the test is correct today, but the helper is a footgun: a future createSubject(otherProfileId, ...) call will silently send user-1 auth with user-2's profile ID, likely returning 403/404 for the wrong reason - making a broken test look like a passing isolation check. The createOwnerProfile refactor (accepting an optional user) is the right model; createSubject should follow the same shape.

title: createHookWrapper` provides a `ProfileContext.Provider` whose default profile has `id: 'test-profile-id'`, but it does **not** call `setActiveProfileId()` to sync the api-client module-level `_activeP
body: `createHookWrapper` provides a `ProfileContext.Provider` whose default profile has `id: 'test-profile-id'`, but it does **not** call `setActiveProfileId()` to sync the api-client module-level `_activeProfileId`. This test works because `beforeEach` manually calls `setActiveProfileId('test-profile-id')`. The coupling is invisible: any future hook test that calls `createHookWrapper()` without also setting `setActiveProfileId` will have a `ProfileContext` with a profile ID but will send requests without the `X-Profile-Id` header. Consider syncing this inside `createHookWrapper` (e.g., `setActiveProfileId(activeProfile?.id)`) to make future tests self-contained.

title: (1) TopicPickerSheet.test.tsx lines 60-74: implicit light-mode dependency without ThemeContext.Provider
body:
(1) TopicPickerSheet.test.tsx lines 60-74: implicit light-mode dependency without ThemeContext.Provider

The two style-asserting tests check tokens.light.colors.primarySoft and tokens.light.colors.surface but never wrap in ThemeContext.Provider. ThemeContext defaults to colorScheme: light so tests pass today, but this is an invisible invariant. If the default ever changes, or a future test provides a dark context, these assertions fail with no obvious cause. LibrarySearchBar.test.tsx in this same PR wraps in an explicit provider for exactly this reason. TopicPickerSheet.test.tsx should do the same for the style-asserting tests.

(2) subject-management.integration.test.ts:76 - unsafe cast discards schema types

'return body.subject as { id: string; name: string }' discards the inferred Zod output type. If the schema shape changes, TypeScript will not catch the mismatch inside createSubject. The return type should be derived from the parsed schema directly rather than cast.

(3) subject-management.integration.test.ts:156 - explicit annotation in .map() hints at a typing gap

'(row: { id: string })' is required because TypeScript cannot infer the element type from the parsed result. This suggests subjectListResponseSchema may not fully propagate the array element type via z.array(). Worth checking so the annotation can be dropped.

title: The job-level comment at line 55 still reads:
body:
The job-level comment at line 55 still reads:
> PR / push-to-main: lightweight lint+typecheck (CI ran full suite on PR).

But after this PR, the `api-quality-gate` job on PRs now also spins up a postgres service container and runs `drizzle-kit migrate` to validate committed migration SQL. Neither of those is lightweight or covered by `ci.yml`. The migration validation is the most valuable new signal for PRs (catches schema drift before it reaches staging), so it should be called out explicitly in the comment. Otherwise the next engineer reading it will assume postgres is free.

pr_title: fix(tests): remove internal mock cleanup debt
title: let queryClient: QueryClient` is typed non-nullable, but `afterEach` uses `queryClient?.clear()` -- optional chaining that only makes sense if the variable can be undefined. The variable is assigned o
body: `let queryClient: QueryClient` is typed non-nullable, but `afterEach` uses `queryClient?.clear()` -- optional chaining that only makes sense if the variable can be undefined. The variable is assigned only inside `createWrapper()`, so if a test throws before `renderHook`, the optional chain silently skips the clear rather than surfacing the bug. Prefer `let queryClient: QueryClient | undefined` so TypeScript catches any unguarded direct access.

body:
**H1: `AppVariables.profileId` typed `string` but middleware sets `string | undefined`**

`profile-scope.ts:51` explicitly declares `profileId: string | undefined`. The new `AppVariables` in `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:11` declares `profileId: string` (no undefined). body:
**H2: `session-operations.integration.test.ts:306` — raw cast papers over a return-type lie**

```ts
const firstMeta = firstResult.metadata as Record<string, unknown>;
```

`syncHomeworkState` is declared to return `Promise<{ metadata: HomeworkSessionMetadata }>` but actually returns fields (`loggedCorrectionIds`, `loggedStartedProblemIds`, `loggedCompletedProblemIds`) absent from `HomeworkSessionMetadata`. The test was correctly asserting those fields before this PR; the fix papers over the type gap with a raw cast instead of fixing the declared return type.

The cast means future callers will trust the too-narrow annotation and silently lose the extra fields. Options: (1) widen the return type of `syncHomeworkState`; (2) add a `// TODO:` comment referencing a tracking issue if deferring. title: M1 — `BASE_ENV: Env` fixture uses post-coercion numeric literals
body:
**M1 — `BASE_ENV: Env` fixture uses post-coercion numeric literals**
`apps/api/src/config.test.ts:28-30`

`MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_THRESHOLD: 0.15`, `MAX_DEDUP_LLM_CALLS_PER_SESSION: 10`, and `MEMORY_FACTS_DEDUP_ROLLOUT_PCT: 0` are JS numbers. In production they arrive as strings from the CF env. `Env` is the Zod output type (post-`z.coerce`), so TypeScript accepts these. The gap: `validateProductionKeys` is never tested with raw string inputs for these keys. A raw string that fails `z.coerce.number()` in a corner case would not be caught. Consider adding one test that pipes a `Record<string, string>` through `validateEnv` to exercise the coercion path.

body:
**M1: `topup-expiry-reminder.test.ts:201,245` — `(args.length as number)` cast is a no-op**

`ConstructorParameters<typeof Date>` is a rest-parameter tuple. In TS strict mode, comparing `.length` to `0` triggers TS2367 because the inferred type is a union of literal lengths. Casting to `number` silences this without addressing it. A future TS upgrade may re-surface the error. Use `!args.length` instead.

title: File**: `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:13
body:
**File**: `apps/api/src/types/hono.ts:13`

`profileId` was correctly fixed to `string | undefined` (CR-1) because the middleware only sets it when a profile resolves. `subscriptionId` has the identical problem: `meteringMiddleware` is the only setter and it is **not registered on every route**. Every test file that imports `Hono<{ Variables: AppVariables }>` without the metering middleware (`account.test.ts`, `profile-scope.test.ts`, `notes.test.ts`, `stripe-webhook.test.ts`) has TypeScript believing `c.get('subscriptionId')` returns `string` when the runtime value is `undefined`.

The prior review deferred this under CR-2 ("partial consolidation") but CR-2 is about fields *not yet merged into AppVariables*; the type-safety issue with fields that ARE present is distinct and the same class as CR-1. Fix: change `subscriptionId: string` to `subscriptionId: string | undefined`, and `llmTier: LLMTier` to `llmTier: LLMTier | undefined` (metering sets both together).

body:
**`docs/project_context.md` frontmatter `project_name` case change may break tooling**

The field changed from `'eduagent'` (lowercase) to `'MentoMate'` (mixed-case). Archon scripts (e.g. `.archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh`) parse this frontmatter to identify the project. If any script, BMAD workflow, or prompt template does a case-sensitive match on this value, the change will silently produce no-match rather than a visible error. Before merging, confirm with:

```bash
grep -r "eduagent" .archon/ _bmad/ --include="*.sh" --include="*.yaml" --include="*.md"
```

title: Unvalidated `subjectId` in dictation and quiz routes
body:
**Unvalidated `subjectId` in dictation and quiz routes**
See inline on `packages/schemas/src/dictation.ts:76`. The `subjectId` field accepted in `POST /v1/dictation/results` (and for non-vocabulary quiz rounds in `orchestrate-round.ts:93`) is stored in `practice_activity_events.subject_id` without verifying the subject belongs to the caller's profile. CLAUDE.md rule: "Writes must include explicit profileId protection or verify ownership through the parent chain." The practical impact is limited (subject name resolves to `null` in summaries via the join's `eq(subjects.profileId, profileId)` condition), but the FK can point to another profile's subject row, which is a data-integrity violation. A one-line scoped-repo ownership check before the write closes this.

title: File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 45
body:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.ts` line 45

`localDate` is a calendar date in the learner's local timezone. Appending `T00:00:00.000Z` pins it to UTC midnight, which misrepresents the event for every learner outside UTC. A learner in UTC+9 who practices on May 13 local time gets a `completedAt` of May 12 15:00 UTC, causing report aggregations to bucket the event into the wrong day.

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: File:** `apps/api/src/services/practice-activity-events.ts` line 32
body:
**File:** `apps/api/src/services/practice-activity-events.ts` line 32

```ts
return `${activityType}:${sourceType}${subtype}:${sourceId}${occurrence}`;
```

When `activitySubtype` is present the subtype is spliced into the string adjacent to `sourceType` without a separator between them. Two different `(sourceType, activitySubtype)` pairs can produce the same key if `sourceType` ever contains a colon. Today's source types are safe, but the format is fragile. A fixed-field scheme with explicit separators for every segment (including a sentinel when `activitySubtype` is null) is more robust.

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: File:** `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts` line 102
body:
**File:** `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts` line 102

`/[?!.:]/.test(text)` returns `true` for any period, which matches abbreviations (`Dr.`), URLs, and ordinary prose. This classifies nearly all multi-word text as having homework cues, suppressing escalation even for weak-confidence results with no actual homework content. Restricting to `?` and `!` only, or requiring whitespace after a period, would reduce false-positive suppression.

---

### 🟢 Low — 90-day scan on Progress tab critical path

**File:** `apps/api/src/services/progress.ts`

`getPracticeActivitySummary` is parallelised with the subjects query, so it does not block the critical path, but a 90-day window will grow as the table fills. Consider reducing to 30 days for the overview and reserving 90 days for report-detail endpoints that are opened intentionally.

---

### 🟢 Low — `emptyProgressOverviewPracticeSummary` duplicates test fixture

`packages/schemas/src/progress.ts` defines this object inline; it is structurally identical to `emptyPracticeActivitySummary` in `apps/api/src/test-utils/practice-activity-summary-fixture.ts`. Exporting one canonical empty from `@eduagent/schemas` and importing it in the test fixture would keep them in sync automatically.

---

### ✅ Strengths
- Migration SQL: idempotent DDL, `NOT VALID` backfill, correct `NULLIF` RLS guard pattern.
- Deduplication via `(profile_id, dedupe_key)` unique index; integration tests verify constraint behaviour directly.
- All `practice_activity_events` writes are inside the parent transaction (dictation, quiz), keeping events atomic with the primary record.
- `@eduagent/schemas` owns all shared types; no local API-facing type redefinitions.
- `unregisterProvider` + `dispose()` on the LLM test fixture cleanly fixes provider leakage between integration tests.
- Locale key-parity test is a good CI guard for i18n drift.

title: Colon-collision in `buildCelebrationDedupeKey`** — see inline on `celebration-events.ts:25
body:
**1. Colon-collision in `buildCelebrationDedupeKey`** — see inline on `celebration-events.ts:25`
The separator character `:` is not encoded, so distinct `(celebrationType, reason, sourceId)` tuples can produce the same key if any field contains a literal colon. `buildPracticeActivityDedupeKey` in the sibling service already uses `encodeURIComponent`. Both helpers should apply the same encoding.

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: Telemetry write inside transaction with no error isolation** — see inline on `assessments.ts:172–178
body:
**2. Telemetry write inside transaction with no error isolation** — see inline on `assessments.ts:172–178`
`recordAssessmentCompletionActivity` is called inside the Drizzle transaction. While `onConflictDoNothing` handles the expected duplicate path, any other unexpected DB failure will roll back the entire assessment update and surface a 500. The session-exchange path wraps the equivalent call with `captureException` and lets the exchange complete. Activity event recording is telemetry; it should not gate the core completion flow.

pr_title: Practice activity reporting and homework OCR migration
title: practice_activity_events.source_type` has no DB-level constraint.** Unlike `celebration_events`, which gets the `source_type_known` CHECK constraint in migration 0074, `practice_activity_events.source
body:
**`practice_activity_events.source_type` has no DB-level constraint.** Unlike `celebration_events`, which gets the `source_type_known` CHECK constraint in migration 0074, `practice_activity_events.source_type` is unconstrained text. Valid values today are `assessment`, `dictation_result`, `quiz_round`, `quiz_mastery_item`, `session_event`. A future caller with a typo will silently insert an invalid source type, breaking any report query that groups or filters on that column. A CHECK constraint (or at minimum a shared constant between the migration and the service layer) would catch this at insert time.

**OCR endpoint buffers the full body before validating size.** `c.req.parseBody()` reads the entire multipart request into worker memory before the `file.size > OCR_CONSTRAINTS.maxFileSizeBytes` check. A client that sends a 50 MB body exhausts CF Worker memory before being rejected. Enforce the limit via a Hono body-size middleware or CF's `limits.fetch_body_size_limit` in `wrangler.toml`. See inline comment on `homework.ts`.

**`NOT VALID` in migration 0074 lacks a rationale comment.** `NOT VALID` is appropriate when a table already has data you cannot afford to scan. `celebration_events` was created in migration 0072 — if you used `NOT VALID` because rows were written on staging between the two deployments, that context should be in a SQL comment so the next reader does not wonder whether existing rows violate the constraint.

**Inconsistent `dedupeKey` format at the `recitation`/`fluency_drill` call-site.** In `session-exchange.ts` the recitation event uses a hand-rolled key that bypasses `buildPracticeActivityDedupeKey`, while the fluency_drill event on the adjacent branch uses the auto-generated format. Both reference the same `aiEventId` and are unique, so there is no correctness bug today, but the two formats diverge in the DB and will confuse anyone auditing or migrating dedupe keys. Pick one approach for both.

---

body:
**Silent practice-event drop has no queryable failure rate** (inline on `apps/api/src/routes/assessments.ts:174`)
The `try/catch` around `recordAssessmentCompletionActivity` fires `captureException` but Sentry exceptions are sampled. Per CLAUDE.md: "if you can't query how many times the fallback fired in the last 24 hours, the 'recovery' is invisible." A tagged metric or an Inngest retry is needed.

**New OCR error string not i18n'd** (inline on `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.ts:399`)
Two occurrences of `"We couldn't read that clearly..."` are hardcoded in English. The PR adds locale files for six languages. These strings must go through the translation system like `NON_HOMEWORK_ERROR_MESSAGE`'s sibling keys.

**Migration 0074 missing rollback section** (inline on `apps/api/drizzle/0074_celebration_events_source_type_check.sql`)
CLAUDE.md requires any migration altering an existing table to have a Rollback section in the plan. This migration adds a `NOT VALID` constraint to `celebration_events`; rollback is straightforward (`DROP CONSTRAINT`) but must be documented before the migration runs on staging.

pr_title: Add child progress summaries and parent surfaces
title: [HIGH] `isAppHelpQuery` regex: first branch is too broad**
body:
**[HIGH] `isAppHelpQuery` regex: first branch is too broad**

The `APP_HELP_FRAMED` regex's opening alternative is `where (do i|are my|can i|is the) (find|see|change|access|get to)\b` with no object constraint. It matches ordinary learning questions: "where do i find the quadratic formula?", "where can i see the Pacific Ocean?", "where do i change the subject in this sentence?". Each would call `applyAppHelpSignalGuard`, zeroing out `partialProgress`, `needsDeepening`, and `understandingCheck` for a genuine exchange. The session never advances. See inline comment on the regex line.

pr_title: Add child progress summaries and parent surfaces
title: [MEDIUM] Idempotency key format change breaks Resend deduplication**
body:
**[MEDIUM] Idempotency key format change breaks Resend deduplication**

`buildEmailIdempotencyKey('weekly', parentId, reportWeek)` emits `value(weekly):value(\<uuid\>):value(2026-05-12)`. The old template literal emitted `weekly-\<uuid\>-2026-05-12`. These are different strings in Resend's dedup store. Any email sent within Resend's idempotency window (~24 h) before deployment could be re-sent immediately after with the new-format key. See inline comment on the call site.

**[LOW] `classifyActivityState`: `stale` silently shadows `no_recent_activity`**

When `latestSessionAt > basedOnLastSessionAt` AND `daysBetween(now, latestSessionAt) >= 2`, the function returns `stale` because that check comes first. The guardian UI shows the generic stale banner but never the inactivity-nudge copy. If this is intentional, a one-line comment prevents future confusion. See inline comment.

**[LOW] `joinDedupeKey` is a dead export**

`dedupe-key.ts` exports `joinDedupeKey` but no caller in this PR uses it. CLAUDE.md: "Don't add features...beyond what the task requires."

**[LOW] Multi-paragraph JSDoc in `dedupe-key.ts`**

The six-line `/** ... */` block spans two paragraphs. CLAUDE.md: "Never write multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks — one short line max."

---

**What is good**

Auth guards (`assertParentAccess` + `assertChildDashboardDataVisible`) are placed before any data access on the new endpoint. LLM prompt injection runs child name and subject names through `sanitizeXmlValue`/`escapeXml`. Debounce key and retry count are appropriate for a post-session fan-out. LLM fallback is stored to the DB so parents always see something, with copy that signals a richer summary is coming. `useChildProgressSummary` correctly gates on `activeProfile.isOwner === true`. All internal `jest.mock` calls in the new Inngest test carry `gc1-allow` annotations. Integration tests cover 200, 403-no-link, 403-consent-withdrawn, and 401.

The function comment says "Do not use for new email types," but the type system will not enforce this. A future developer calling `buildLegacyEmailIdempotencyKey("feedback", ...)` gets no compile-time warning. Narrowing to `'weekly' | 'monthly'` makes misuse a type error and communicates intent more forcefully than a comment alone.

pr_title: fix(api): address post-merge review findings
title: Adding `progress` to the noun list introduces a boundary case: "Where can I see progress on this topic?" or "Where can I see my progress in the textbook?" would now be classified as app-help (routing
body:
Adding `progress` to the noun list introduces a boundary case: "Where can I see progress on this topic?" or "Where can I see my progress in the textbook?" would now be classified as app-help (routing to the Progress tab) when the learner is asking a genuine content question. The positive test case `'Where can I see my progress?'` is itself ambiguous — it is equally valid as a learning question to the tutor. Consider either narrowing the pattern (`progress (tab|page|screen|section)?`) or restricting the `progress` keyword to the narrower `where are/is/can i find` branch only. At minimum, add a negative test such as `'Where can I see progress on this topic?'` to confirm the regex rejects it.

---

title: The `hasAdminTagInLeadingComments` function (L59–69 of `inngest-admin-tag.mjs`) calls `getAllComments()`, which returns every comment in the file, including those buried inside function bodies. A deve
body: The `hasAdminTagInLeadingComments` function (L59–69 of `inngest-admin-tag.mjs`) calls `getAllComments()`, which returns every comment in the file, including those buried inside function bodies. A developer can write `// @inngest-admin: cross-profile` inside an `if` block or helper function and the file-level check passes — defeating the point of a visible preamble declaration. Inline comment posted with a suggested cutoff fix. Test suite has no case that exercises a tag inside a function body, so this is currently unverified.

body: The JSDoc names the conditional-branch limitation but the same walk also descends into FunctionExpression / ArrowFunctionExpression nodes. A router.push(parent) inside a callback (e.g. a .forEach handler or an onPress prop) would satisfy the check for the outer function even though that push never runs before the outer call. See inline comment for a one-line guard fix. Not blocking since 0 violations exist today, but worth closing before the rule catches real cross-stack pushes in review.

title: GC1 lint bypass in ShelfRow.test.tsx - wrong gc1-allow placement
body:
**1. GC1 lint bypass in ShelfRow.test.tsx - wrong gc1-allow placement**

The ESLint rule (`eslint-rules/no-internal-jest-mock.mjs`) checks the source line of `jest.mock(` OR the line of the first string argument. The `gc1-allow` annotation lands inside the factory body on line 10 - neither line the rule inspects. The warning will fire and block CI lint.

Fix: move the annotation to the `jest.mock(` line:
```
jest.mock( // gc1-allow: ThemeProvider requires native env; unit test cannot render it
  '../../lib/theme',
  () => ...
);
```

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: @tanstack/react-query-persist-client peer dependency version mismatch
body:
**2. @tanstack/react-query-persist-client peer dependency version mismatch**

The persist client at 5.100.10 declares `peerDependency @tanstack/react-query ^5.100.10`, but the project resolves `@tanstack/react-query@5.90.21` (backed by `query-core@5.90.20`). The lockfile ends up with two different `query-core` instances: 5.90.20 inside react-query and 5.100.10 inside the persist client. `PersistQueryClientProvider` receives a `QueryClient` built against the 5.90 core while its own internals expect the 5.100 API - subscriber callbacks and cache-buster logic can fail silently or throw at runtime.

Fix: bump `@tanstack/react-query` to `^5.100.10` in `apps/mobile/package.json` so both packages resolve a single `query-core` version.

title: Query cache persistence stores sensitive data in unencrypted AsyncStorage
body:
**5. Query cache persistence stores sensitive data in unencrypted AsyncStorage**

`query-persister.ts` persists the full TanStack Query cache (child profiles, session transcripts, dashboard data) to `AsyncStorage`, which is unencrypted on both platforms. For an app processing educational data for minors (COPPA/GDPR-K territory), this warrants at minimum an inline comment acknowledging the threat model and why it is acceptable (data is non-credential, OS sandbox is the boundary).

title: packages/database/src/client.ts: connection string used as Map key holds credentials verbatim
body:
**6. packages/database/src/client.ts: connection string used as Map key holds credentials verbatim**

`neonPoolCache` is keyed by the full `DATABASE_URL` including credentials. While this is in-process (not a network leak), any heap snapshot exposes the credential as a plain string key. Consider keying by `sha256(databaseUrl)` to reduce surface area without changing semantics.

title: Looks Good
body:
**Looks Good**

- Inngest test utilities (inngest-step-runner.ts, inngest-transport-capture.ts): clean, well-typed; shifting from `toHaveBeenCalledWith` to `toContainEqual` makes assertions order-independent and far easier to read.
- query-keys.ts registry: key-identity snapshot tests provide a strong regression net; the `children` vs `child` inconsistency is correctly flagged with a deferred-normalisation note.
- CI nx affected (ci.yml): correctly guarded behind a PR-only condition; the pre-existing `fetch-depth: 0` and explicit base-branch fetch step make `NX_BASE` safe.
- Palette trim (8 to 5): breaking the TypeScript union type means stale references are caught at compile time. Test updates are correct.
- react-native-fit-image jest shim: root cause explanation is accurate; follows the same pattern as the existing nativewind shim; correctly unblocks the four chat/session test suites.
- ChildCommandCard routing split: separating header to profile detail from button to progress is the right UX model; both routes have test coverage.
- neonPoolCache: comment explains the cold-start motivation clearly; implementation is correct for Cloudflare Worker module scope.

title: Lines 26 and 60 of `SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx` hardcode `rgba(255,255,255,0.72)` (container background) and `rgba(255,255,255,0.42)` (spine shine strip). CLAUDE.md requires shared mobile components to
body:
Lines 26 and 60 of `SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx` hardcode `rgba(255,255,255,0.72)` (container background) and `rgba(255,255,255,0.42)` (spine shine strip). CLAUDE.md requires shared mobile components to use semantic tokens, not hardcoded colours. The exceptions apply only to `*Animation.tsx`, `*Celebration.tsx`, `AnimatedSplash.tsx`, `MentomateLogo.tsx` — not this component.

On a dark theme, `rgba(255,255,255,0.72)` renders as an opaque white box over the dark background. The container background should use a semantic surface token (e.g. `colors.cardBackground`), and the shine highlight can use `tint.soft` or an opacity variant.

body: Every other test file adopting `createInngestTransportCapture` in this PR calls `mockInngestTransport.clear()` in `beforeEach` (`daily-reminder-send.test.ts`, `email-digest-channel.test.ts`, `feedback-delivery-failed.test.ts`). title: The new error condition is `const combinedError = !hasAnyData && isError` where `isError` is the monthly reports error only. `weeklyError` was removed from the `useChildWeeklyReports` destructure. If
body: The new error condition is `const combinedError = !hasAnyData && isError` where `isError` is the monthly reports error only. `weeklyError` was removed from the `useChildWeeklyReports` destructure. If weekly reports fail while monthly succeed, `combinedError` is `false` and the user sees neither an error banner nor a retry for the weekly endpoint. The old code showed an explicit "Could not load weekly snapshots" block with its own retry. This is a regression against the UX resilience rules (CLAUDE.md: "Primary action retries / fixes the specific problem"). Minimal fix: `const combinedError = !hasAnyData && (isError || weeklyError)`.

title: return useMemo(() => ..., [data?.subjects])` — React Query returns a new array reference for `data.subjects` on every background refetch even when data is structurally unchanged. The memo recomputes a
body: `return useMemo(() => ..., [data?.subjects])` — React Query returns a new array reference for `data.subjects` on every background refetch even when data is structurally unchanged. The memo recomputes and returns a new Map on each refetch, re-rendering every consumer. Change the dependency to `data` — `useQuery` stabilises the top-level `data` object reference via structural sharing; individual sub-properties do not carry that guarantee.

title: apps/api/src/routes/resend-webhook.ts` — dedup logic
body:
`apps/api/src/routes/resend-webhook.ts` — dedup logic

The `get -> process -> put` sequence is not atomic. Two concurrent requests with the same `svix-id` arriving within the same millisecond will both pass the `get()` check before either completes the `put()`. Cloudflare KV has no atomic test-and-set primitive.

A tighter approach: move the `put()` to **before** processing (optimistic write), which narrows the race to the sub-millisecond KV propagation window rather than the full handler execution time. Risk is low in practice (Resend does not retry 2xx), but the guard's correctness guarantee is weaker than the comment implies. At minimum, document this limitation in the code comment block.

title: Files: apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts — processMessage catch (~line 1863) and both streamMessage catch blocks (~lines 2043, 2092)
body:
Files: apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts — processMessage catch (~line 1863) and both streamMessage catch blocks (~lines 2043, 2092)

The original code called captureException(persistErr, ...) unconditionally every time orphan persistence failed, giving Sentry a full exception object with stack trace. After this migration, Sentry only fires if the Inngest dispatch itself fails (inside safeSend). The underlying persistErr — the actual failure that triggered the orphan-persist path — is no longer sent to Sentry at all. Its .toString() string is embedded in the Inngest event payload, but that means no stack trace, no fingerprint, no queryable Sentry issue for orphan-persist failures.

CLAUDE.md: 'Silent recovery without escalation is banned.' The Inngest event satisfies the escalation requirement, but the Sentry observability regression is real. Each of the three sites should add an explicit captureException(persistErr, { extra: { phase: 'orphan_persist_failed', ... } }) call alongside safeSend. See the inline comment on lines 1863-1878 for a suggested diff.

title: See inline comment on `safe-non-core.guard.test.ts` lines 99-119. The opening comment says "Stop at function boundaries" but the if-block is empty - the walk unconditionally continues across `ArrowFun
body: See inline comment on `safe-non-core.guard.test.ts` lines 99-119. The opening comment says "Stop at function boundaries" but the if-block is empty - the walk unconditionally continues across `ArrowFunction`/`FunctionDeclaration` ancestors. An `inngest.send()` inside a nested arrow function defined inside a try-catch passes the ratchet as `try-catch` even though the outer try does not guard its actual execution. Fix: add `return false;` inside the if-block.

pr_title: refactor(api): finish telemetry isolation sweep + forward-only ratchet
title: See inline comment on `session-exchange.ts` lines 1863-1879 (same pattern at the `stream` and `fallback` catch sites). The original code reported `persistErr` directly to Sentry with a full stack trac
body: See inline comment on `session-exchange.ts` lines 1863-1879 (same pattern at the `stream` and `fallback` catch sites). The original code reported `persistErr` directly to Sentry with a full stack trace AND dispatched an Inngest event. The new code only dispatches the Inngest event; `captureException` now fires only if the dispatch itself fails. If Inngest is unavailable, `persistErr` disappears from Sentry entirely. Per CLAUDE.md "Silent recovery without escalation is banned" - the Inngest event satisfies the structured-event requirement, but losing direct Sentry escalation for these three error-within-error catch sites is a regression. Suggest adding `captureException(persistErr)` before `safeSend` at all three orphan-persist sites.

`captureExceptionSpy.mockRestore()` is called at line 1337. Jest's `mockRestore()` chains through `mockReset()` then `mockClear()`, which zeroes out `mock.calls`. title: Spy leak in `memory dedup rollout` test** (`session-completed.integration.test.ts:1239-1272`)
body:
**Spy leak in `memory dedup rollout` test** (`session-completed.integration.test.ts:1239-1272`)

`dedupEnabledSpy` and `rolloutSpy` are inline-restored after `handler()`. If `handler()` throws, both spies remain active (`.mockReturnValue(true)`) for all subsequent tests. `jest.clearAllMocks()` in `beforeEach` clears call counts but does NOT restore spy implementations — so a flaky upstream failure would silently corrupt the dedup-rollout flag for every later test. Wrap in `try/finally` or add `afterEach(() => jest.restoreAllMocks())` to the outer describe. See inline comment.

body:
**1. gc1-allow reason uniformly erased (spans entire PR)**

Every pre-existing annotation carried a reason explaining why the boundary needs a mock, such as "external-boundary - DB-dependent; calculateAge is real", "isolates unit test from DB-backed filing service", "prevents real LLM calls while asserting topic generation boundary", and "prevents destructive profile deletion while asserting the handler boundary". All have been replaced with the generic "pattern-a conversion". The ESLint rule only checks for presence of gc1-allow, not quality of the reason, so this passes lint - but it removes the audit trail for future contributors: is this mock still necessary? What breaks if removed? Which destructive-operation concern does it guard?

Recommendation: Append the boundary description, e.g. gc1-allow: pattern-a conversion - prevents real LLM calls. Cheap to fix, high value for maintainability.

pr_title: refactor(test): GC1 internal jest.mock() drain — 24 batches, ~300 mock sites eliminated
title: Opt-in to opt-out mock semantics (multiple files)
body:
**2. Opt-in to opt-out mock semantics (multiple files)**

Before this PR, many mocks were opt-in: the factory returned only the exports the test needed. If the SUT accidentally called a non-listed function it received undefined - a fast, obvious failure.

After Pattern A, mocks are opt-out: spreading actual exposes every real export, then specific ones are overridden. If the SUT calls a non-overridden DB-backed function it now runs the real implementation against the absent test database rather than failing immediately.

Examples where non-overridden real functions are now silently live:
- consent-revocation.test.ts (services/deletion): only deleteProfile overridden; any other deletion function is now real.
- consent-revocation.test.ts (services/notices): only recordPendingNotice overridden.
- freeform-filing.test.ts (services/filing): only three functions overridden; filingExists, loadFilingState, etc. are now real and DB-backed.

These all pass today because the SUT happens not to call non-overridden functions. But the safety net is gone - a future refactor adding a service call would silently exercise a real DB path rather than failing loudly.

Recommendation: For any service mock where the real module makes DB or external calls, add a short inline comment listing which functions are intentionally exposed from the actual spread. Makes the isolation contract explicit for the next reader.

---

pr_title: fix(profile): restore first profile and consent flows
title: ConsentManagementSection silently disappears on query error. When consent.isError is true (network failure, server error, 403), hasConsentRecord resolves to false (loading=false, consentStatus=null) a
body:
ConsentManagementSection silently disappears on query error. When consent.isError is true (network failure, server error, 403), hasConsentRecord resolves to false (loading=false, consentStatus=null) and the section unmounts silently. A parent with a transient network error sees the consent UI vanish with no message and no retry button. This violates the UX Resilience rule in CLAUDE.md (Standard error fallback pattern). See inline comments on index.tsx for a two-part fix.

POSITIVES

cacheNeonPool: false - Correct fix for workerd request-context isolation. Cloudflare binds WebSocket handles to the request context that created them; a module-level pool from request A is rejected in request B. Test verifies the new call signature.

setQueriesData + duplicate guard in create-profile.tsx - Correctly updates scoped query keys like ['profiles', clerkId] (old setQueryData only hit the un-scoped key) and guards against double-insertion on concurrent calls.

assertOk before res.json() is safe - assertOk reads .text() only when res.ok is false, in which case assertOk throws and res.json() is never reached. Body stream consumed at most once.

refetchQueries over invalidateQueries in refreshConsentGate - Better for the 'Check again' button; refetchQueries fires immediately regardless of active subscribers, which is the intent.

respondedAt anchor confirmed correct - consent.ts line 666 shows revokeConsent sets respondedAt = now at revocation time. Grace-period countdown starts from the right anchor.

GC1/GC6 compliance clean - All new internal jest.mock calls carry gc1-allow annotations with coherent justifications.

MINOR (non-blocking)

daysRemaining is computed unconditionally even when consentStatus is not WITHDRAWN. When status is CONSENTED, respondedAt holds the original grant timestamp so getGracePeriodDaysRemaining returns 0. No user-visible bug (value never displayed in non-withdrawn branch), but moving the computation inside the isWithdrawn branch would clarify intent.

title: Plural regression in `deletionGraceDays` (all 7 locale files) — see inline comment on `en.json`. The key passes `count` but lacks `_one`/`_other` variants; i18next will render "1 days left to cancel d
body: Plural regression in `deletionGraceDays` (all 7 locale files) — see inline comment on `en.json`. The key passes `count` but lacks `_one`/`_other` variants; i18next will render "1 days left to cancel deletion." for the last day in English, and grammatically wrong singular forms in every other locale (e.g. German "1 Tage" instead of "1 Tag"). Every other count-bearing key in the project uses the suffix pattern (`sessions_one`/`sessions_other`, `childCount_one`/`childCount_other`, etc.). The `defaultValue` ternary in the component does **not** rescue this — it only fires when the key is absent from the namespace, which it is not.

pr_title: fix(subject): restore book generation flow
title: LLM call inside the polling loop is not deadline-bounded
body:
**1. LLM call inside the polling loop is not deadline-bounded**

`startFirstCurriculumSession` checks `Date.now() <= deadline` only at the top of each while iteration. Once `materializeFocusedBookTopics` is entered, the `generateBookTopics` LLM call runs to completion with no timeout guard. If the LLM takes longer than 25 s (`FIRST_CURRICULUM_SESSION_WAIT_MS`), the topics ARE persisted to the DB, but the deadline has already lapsed when `continue` loops back — the function exits and throws `CurriculumSessionNotReadyError` despite successful materialization. Users who retry immediately succeed because the data is already there, making this a confusing false-negative failure.

Fix options:
- After `materializeFocusedBookTopics` succeeds, do one unconditional `findFirstAvailableTopicId` poll in the same iteration before continuing, bypassing the deadline gate for this one step.
- Or pass an `AbortSignal` derived from the remaining deadline into `generateBookTopics` / `routeAndCall`.

pr_title: fix(session): stabilize core learning session flow
title: File:** `.test-receipts/mobile.json
body:
**File:** `.test-receipts/mobile.json`

The `command` field in the receipt is a manually composed jest invocation with `--runTestsByPath` restricting the run to a hand-picked subset of files. `record-test-receipt.sh` **always** writes `command: "pnpm exec nx run mobile:test"` — that string is hardcoded in the script (lines 56 / 123). Any receipt that contains a different command string was not generated by the canonical script.

The commit message for `e1754cf` confirms the reason: *"unrelated local OCR suite apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.test.ts failed 7 tests because server OCR fetch was not invoked."* The failing file was excluded from `--runTestsByPath` so the subset run could record `"passed": true`.

**This is the exact failure mode the receipt system was built to prevent** (cf. PR #257 / 189 broken tests hidden by a partial CI step). The receipt must be regenerated by running `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` after the `use-homework-ocr` failures are resolved. If those failures are pre-existing flakiness that only surfaces without a real server, that should be investigated and fixed or properly annotated — not excluded silently.

body:
**File:** `.agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md`

The change rewrites:
> *"Never push unless the user explicitly says 'push' or 'commit and push'."*

to:
> *"Push after successful commits unless the user explicitly says not to push."*

This opt-out-of-push model conflicts with the principle that actions visible to others require explicit confirmation and that one-time approval does not generalize to all contexts. Auto-push after every commit will push WIP commits and shared PR branches without an explicit user signal. If the intent is to shorten the push round-trip in automated agent workflows, consider scoping the change to a flag or separate skill rather than changing the default for all invocations.

title: getDeletionStatus` throws a plain `Error`, not a typed `NotFoundError` (see inline comment on `deletion.ts:92`). The global `onError` handler converts `NotFoundError` → 404 cleanly; a plain `Error` fa
body:
`getDeletionStatus` throws a plain `Error`, not a typed `NotFoundError` (see inline comment on `deletion.ts:92`). The global `onError` handler converts `NotFoundError` → 404 cleanly; a plain `Error` falls through to the catch-all, fires `captureException`, and returns 500. In production `accountMiddleware` should always resolve a valid account, but a Clerk-to-local-DB sync gap makes this reachable. The same gap affects `POST /account/delete` because `scheduleDeletion` calls `getDeletionStatus` internally. Fix: use the `NotFoundError` from the typed error hierarchy used elsewhere in the services layer.

body:
**Blanket `catch {}` in `GET /account/deletion-status` maps every error to 404**
(inline comment on `apps/api/src/routes/account.ts` lines 30–33)

The bare `catch {}` swallows database errors, connection timeouts, and Zod parse errors, all returning `404 Account not found`. Only the `Error('account not found: …')` sentinel thrown by `getDeletionStatus` should produce a 404; all other errors should re-throw so the global error handler can capture them in Sentry and return 500. The companion route test only mocks the "account not found" path, so this wouldn't be caught by the existing test suite.

pr_title: fix(account-billing): address small isolated flows
title: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 41-46.
body:
apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts lines 41-46.

After the UPDATE returns empty (deletion already active), scheduleDeletion calls getDeletionStatus in a separate round-trip. In the narrow window between those two DB calls a concurrent cancelDeletion write could flip the row so getDeletionStatus returns { scheduled: false }. The function then falls through to throw new Error('account deletion scheduling failed'), giving the user a 500. This is the same TOCTOU class that deleteProfileIfNoConsent prevents with an atomic DELETE...WHERE NOT EXISTS (line 167). Simplest fix: retry the UPDATE once rather than read-then-throw.

pr_title: fix(account-billing): address small isolated flows
title: apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts line 26 (SQL WHERE) vs line 88 (TypeScript getDeletionStatus).
body:
apps/api/src/services/deletion.ts line 26 (SQL WHERE) vs line 88 (TypeScript getDeletionStatus).

The SQL WHERE uses strict > for the cancellation guard; getDeletionStatus uses strict <. If deletionCancelledAt === deletionScheduledAt (same millisecond - plausible with clock skew or in tests), the UPDATE skips (> is false) and getDeletionStatus returns scheduled: false (< is also false), so scheduleDeletion throws "scheduling failed" and the user gets a 500. Changing the UPDATE WHERE to >= closes the gap and aligns semantics with the TypeScript side.

The SQL WHERE uses strict `>` for the cancellation guard; `getDeletionStatus` uses strict `<`. If `deletionCancelledAt === deletionScheduledAt` (same millisecond — plausible with clock skew or in tests), the UPDATE skips (`>` is false) and `getDeletionStatus` returns `scheduled: false` (`<` is also false), so `scheduleDeletion` throws "scheduling failed" and the user gets a 500. pr_title: fix(account-billing): address small isolated flows
title: The ref is set to `true` synchronously inside `onConfirmDelete` to prevent the `useEffect` from bouncing the user back to `'initial'` when the status query hasn't caught up yet. But the effect's `sche
body: The ref is set to `true` synchronously inside `onConfirmDelete` to prevent the `useEffect` from bouncing the user back to `'initial'` when the status query hasn't caught up yet. But the effect's `scheduled === true` branch immediately resets it to `false` — *before* a subsequent refetch can return a stale `false`. After that reset the guard no longer provides any protection. The exact scenario: POST succeeds → user sees 'scheduled' → GET /deletion-status resolves `true` → effect runs, sets ref to `false` → a second background refetch (window-focus, StrictMode double-invocation, etc.) returns `false` transiently → second branch fires and resets stage to `'initial'`. In practice this requires inconsistent reads, but the guard was designed to prevent exactly this and is silently neutered. See inline comment for the suggested fix.

body:
`deletion.ts:50` uses `>=` to decide whether to allow a re-schedule, but both `isDeletionCancelled` (line 80) and `getDeletionStatus` (line 98) use strict `>` / `<`. If `deletionCancelledAt` and `deletionScheduledAt` land on the same millisecond (possible under load or in time-frozen tests), the SQL treats the row as "cancelled" and overwrites `deletionScheduledAt`, while all JS read functions continue to report `scheduled: true`. The re-schedule silently succeeds but reads report stale state until the next refetch. Change `>=` to `>` to match the strict inequality used everywhere else.

body:
`getDeletionStatus` throws with the accountId in the message when the row is missing (deletion.ts lines 87–88). The new route handler has no try-catch, so if the account is removed between the auth-middleware check and this handler (concurrent delete race), Hono's global error handler returns a 500 whose body may contain the raw error string including the internal accountId. The fix is to catch and map to a generic response.

title: useDeletionStatus` has no explicit `staleTime` (inline comment on `use-account.ts:57`). React Query defaults to staleTime 0, so every mount and app foreground fires a background refetch, which runs th
body:
`useDeletionStatus` has no explicit `staleTime` (inline comment on `use-account.ts:57`). React Query defaults to staleTime 0, so every mount and app foreground fires a background refetch, which runs through the `useEffect` stage-transition logic. The `locallyScheduledRef` guard prevents the worst race but the aggressive revalidation is unintentional. Recommend `staleTime: 30_000`.

`scheduleDeletion`'s terminal error is unclassified (inline comment on `deletion.ts:34`). If both UPDATE attempts return 0 rows and both fallback reads report scheduled: false, the plain `Error` becomes an untyped 500 with no Sentry context. Consider `ConflictError` (409 to client) or at minimum a `captureException` call.

pr_title: fix(account-billing): address small isolated flows
title: stage` in `useEffect` dep array (`delete-account.tsx` line 71)
body:
**`stage` in `useEffect` dep array (`delete-account.tsx` line 71)**
The effect calls `setStage` and lists `stage` as a dependency, so every stage transition re-runs it. React's same-value bail-out keeps it from looping today, but this is the classic pattern that breaks under concurrent rendering or when a future edit removes one of the guard conditions. Removing `stage` from the dep array (and gating writes via a ref or `useReducer`) is the safer path.

title: Bug 1 - SSE done-path: close failure incorrectly surfaces as a stream error (database.ts lines 60-63, inline comment posted)
body:
Bug 1 - SSE done-path: close failure incorrectly surfaces as a stream error (database.ts lines 60-63, inline comment posted)

When done === true, await closeOnce() sits bare inside the try block. If closeDatabase rejects, the outer catch(err) intercepts the close error — not a stream read error — and passes it to controller.error(err). This makes a fully-delivered SSE response appear broken to the client even though all data was sent. Fix: attach .catch(captureException) on the closeOnce() call in the done path so close failures go to Sentry and controller.close() is always reached. A test for "close fails after successful SSE consumption" should accompany the fix — it is the only gap in the otherwise complete database.test.ts coverage.

Bug 2 - SSE cancel-path: close failure escapes Sentry (database.ts lines 75-81, inline comment posted)

finally { await closeOnce() } in cancel() has no .catch(), so a closeDatabase rejection surfaces as an unhandled promise rejection and bypasses Sentry — inconsistent with the error-path handling in pull(). Fix: wrap closeOnce() with .catch(captureException) here too.

pr_title: fix(e2e): P5a — custom domain + web server stabilisation for parallel Playwright workers
title: Both the PR body and `docs/audit/e2e/p5-execution-status.md` document the local multi-worker flow as:
body:
Both the PR body and `docs/audit/e2e/p5-execution-status.md` document the local multi-worker flow as:

```bash
node apps/mobile/e2e-web/helpers/serve-exported-web.mjs &
CI=1 PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_LOCAL_API=1 ... pnpm run test:e2e:web --workers=2
```

But `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI` evaluates to `false` when `CI=1`. Playwright's `reuseExistingServer: false` throws if the URL is already responding — it will spawn `serve-exported-web.mjs`, hit EADDRINUSE, call `process.exit(1)`, and abort before any test runs.

The command must omit `CI=1`:

```bash
PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_LOCAL_API=1 doppler run --project mentomate --config stg -- \
  pnpm run test:e2e:web --workers=2
```

Without `CI=1`, `reuseExistingServer: true` kicks in, Playwright sees the URL already responding, and reuses the external server. The `--workers=2` CLI flag still takes effect. (See inline comment on `playwright.config.ts:73`.)

---

### 🟡 Should-fix: `require()` in `runtime.ts` is untyped and fragile

`runtime.ts` uses a bare `require('./e2e-defaults.js')` (line 20), which only works because Playwright's esbuild compilation injects `require` into TypeScript files. This is invisible to the TypeScript compiler — the `as string` cast on line 23 is the tell. `serve-exported-web.mjs` solves this correctly with `createRequire`.

Simplest fix: convert `e2e-defaults.js` to `e2e-defaults.ts` (it's 7 lines) and use a real `import`. This eliminates the mixed-module shim and the cast. (See inline comment on `runtime.ts:20`.)

---

### 🟡 Should-fix: `import` declaration placed mid-file in `.mjs`

`import { createRequire } from 'node:module'` appears at line 119, after ~115 lines of executable code. ESM hoisting makes this work at runtime, but it will confuse any reader scanning imports. Move to line 1 with the other Node.js imports. (See inline comment on `serve-exported-web.mjs:119`.)

---

### Non-blocking notes

- **`statSync` in the HTTP handler** blocks the event loop on every request. The inline comment explains the tradeoff accurately — for a single-machine local test server with ~30 sequential requests, the event-loop cost is negligible. No action needed, but keeping the comment is the right call.
- **Log files in `docs/audit/e2e/runs/`** (14 files, ~12k lines total) will stay in git history forever. They are useful evidence for the audit trail, but consider whether a linked Notion page or CI artefact would be a better long-term home.
- **`manual-learner-ux.config.ts`** also uses `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI` and will inherit correct local behaviour automatically since it reads `apiBaseUrl` from `runtime.ts`. No changes needed there, but worth knowing the blast radius of any future refactor of this pattern.

title: runtime.ts uses bare require('./e2e-defaults.js') while serve-exported-web.mjs correctly uses createRequire(import.meta.url). This works today because Playwright compiles .ts files via its own CommonJ
body:
runtime.ts uses bare require('./e2e-defaults.js') while serve-exported-web.mjs correctly uses createRequire(import.meta.url). This works today because Playwright compiles .ts files via its own CommonJS transform, but it is inconsistent and fragile:

- Bare require is not defined in the ESM type surface ("module": "esnext" in tsconfig.base.json). The file is excluded from tsconfig.app.json so this escapes type-checking.
- The as string cast on line 23 exists solely to paper over the missing type information from the untyped require call.
- Any future loader change that runs runtime.ts as ESM (e.g., tsx in ESM mode, Playwright ESM mode) will produce a ReferenceError: require is not defined at startup.

**Fix:** Mirror the .mjs pattern — add createRequire to runtime.ts, or convert e2e-defaults.js to e2e-defaults.ts with a named export and use a normal import.
