title: The CI workflow defaults PLAYWRIGHT_API_URL to https://mentomate-api-stg.zwizzly.workers.dev, so today usesSharedStagingApi is true and CI stays at 1 worker. However, if a repo admin changes the E2E_A
body:
The CI workflow defaults PLAYWRIGHT_API_URL to https://mentomate-api-stg.zwizzly.workers.dev, so today usesSharedStagingApi is true and CI stays at 1 worker. However, if a repo admin changes the E2E_API_URL GitHub variable to https://api-test.mentomate.com, CI silently escalates from 1 worker to 4 — no code change, no diff to review. The PR body documents this as intentional, but .github/workflows/e2e-web.yml does not, so the next person touching that variable won't see the warning.

**Recommendation:** Add a comment to e2e-web.yml documenting that switching E2E_API_URL from *.workers.dev to the custom domain implicitly enables 4 workers and is intentional.

pr_title: fix(api): protect visible question quota
title: When shouldRefundAfterHandler is true, safeRefundQuota is called and the middleware returns early
body:
File: `apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts`
Line: ~L453
Issue: When `shouldRefundAfterHandler` is true, `safeRefundQuota` is called and the middleware returns early — skipping the KV cache update block. The KV still reflects the decremented count until TTL expires. For a user who lands at 0 remaining after decrement, the stale KV will falsely return `QUOTA_EXCEEDED` on the next request even though the DB has been refunded.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules (writes must include explicit profileId protection; by extension, quota state must stay consistent across all persistence layers)
Suggested Fix: After `safeRefundQuota` resolves, either (a) also call the same KV invalidation/update path used on success (passing `remainingMonthly + 1`, `remainingTopUp + 1` from the decrement result), or (b) delete the KV key so the next request falls back to the DB read. If `safeRefundQuota` already handles KV cleanup internally, add a comment stating that and close the concern.

pr_title: fix(api): protect visible question quota
title: After safeRefundQuota the middleware returns early, skipping the writeSubscriptionStatus KV update.
body:
File: `apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts`
Line: L464–L480
Issue: After `safeRefundQuota` the middleware `return`s early, skipping the `writeSubscriptionStatus` KV update. The KV will continue to show the decremented (pre-refund) quota until TTL expires. A user near their limit who hits a bad-request error will see false quota-exceeded on the next valid request.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules (KV cache is the fast quota path)
Suggested Fix: After `await safeRefundQuota(...)`, call `writeSubscriptionStatus` with the refunded counts (or at minimum invalidate the key with `kv.delete(...)`) before returning, mirroring the pattern used on the success path.

title: getTableSection regex (?=^\[
body:
File: `apps/api/src/wrangler-config.test.ts`
Line: L278–287
Issue: `getTableSection` regex `(?=^\[
Rule: $)` with the `m` flag causes `$` to match at end of the section header line (immediately before the `\n` that follows `]`). The lazy `([\s\S]*?)` captures `""` at that position, so `section[1]` is always an empty string (falsy) and the function always throws `Missing wrangler.toml section [...]`. Both CFG-13 tests fail with an exception on every run — including when the config is correct — so the guard never provides a meaningful signal.
Suggested Fix: CLAUDE.md § Required Validation

Suggested Fix: Add a `solidAlpha20` (or similar pre-computed alpha variant) to the `SubjectTint` type in `design-tokens.ts`, or introduce a tiny hex-to-rgba utility (`hexWithAlpha(solid, 0.2)`). Both approaches move the format contract into the type system.

pr_title: feat(hooks): add blocking pre-push hook with delta-based validation
title: nx affected -t test --base=HEAD --exclude=mobile
body:
File: `scripts/pre-push-tests.sh`
Line: L133
Issue: `nx affected -t test --base=HEAD --exclude=mobile` — when pre-push runs, all commits are already in HEAD. `--base=HEAD` diffs HEAD against itself, finds zero changed files, and silently reports success. The fallback that is supposed to run broad tests for large deltas (>100 TS files) is effectively a no-op and **fails open**.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Required Validation — "Do not call work complete if related tests, lint, typecheck, or required migrations are still failing." / broken functionality
Suggested Fix: Replace `--base=HEAD` with a meaningful base. The simplest fix that matches the surrounding delta logic: pass the computed remote SHA or `origin/main`. E.g. `NX_DAEMON=false pnpm exec nx affected -t test --base=origin/main --head="$local_sha" --exclude=mobile` — or capture the base SHA computed in Phase 1 and pass it here.

pr_title: Harden learning flow gates
title: The lock window uses [card.updatedAt.getTime() - 1, card.updatedAt.getTime() + 1).
body:
File: `apps/api/src/services/retention-data.ts`
Line: `updateRetentionFromSession` optimistic lock
Issue: The lock window uses `[card.updatedAt.getTime() - 1, card.updatedAt.getTime() + 1)`. The lower bound (`t - 1ms`) unnecessarily accepts DB timestamps that are 1ms earlier than the value that was read, widening the window to 2ms. The rationale (JS millisecond rounding vs. Postgres microseconds) only requires the upper half `[t, t+1)` to cover sub-millisecond precision. The extra `-1` on the lower bound can allow a spurious lock match if a concurrent write completed 1ms before this read.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules — writes must include explicit `profileId` protection; data integrity
Suggested Fix: Use `>= card.updatedAt.getTime()` for the lower bound: `sql\`${retentionCards.updatedAt} >= ${t} AND ${retentionCards.updatedAt} < ${t + 1}\``

pr_title: Harden learning flow gates
title: The batch query fetches all historical snapshots for all profile IDs with no date-range filter.
body:
File: `apps/api/src/services/dashboard.ts`
Line: `buildChildProgressSummariesBatch`
Issue: The batch query fetches all historical snapshots for all profile IDs with no date-range filter. The previous `getLatestSnapshotOnOrBefore()` used `LIMIT 1`. Over time a child with months of daily snapshots will return a large unbounded result set; memory de-duplication in JS is not a substitute for a bounded query.
Rule: docs/project_context.md § Database & Data Rules — Drizzle relational queries for CRUD
Suggested Fix: Add a date filter to keep the window bounded: `AND snapshotDate >= <two weeks ago>`. Only the latest and a prior week snapshot are used, so full history is unnecessary
